markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Jerome Powell out from Fed Board by December 31?
markets.description:

This market is not limited to Jerome Powell’s current position as chair of the Federal Reserve. If Jerome Powell ceases to be Chair of the Federal Reserve, but remains a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors, this will not qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell announced on April 29, 2026, following the FOMC meeting where rates were held steady, that he will remain on the Fed Board of Governors after his chairmanship expires on May 15, despite intense pressure from President Trump, who threatened dismissal and advanced a DOJ probe now halted. Powell's separate governor term extends through January 2028, positioning him to serve in a low-profile role amid transition uncertainties, including Senate confirmation of nominee Kevin Warsh as next chair. This decision underscores Fed independence amid hawkish policy divides, with traders monitoring upcoming June FOMC deliberations and potential legal challenges that could accelerate his exit, reflecting aggregated sentiment on monetary policy continuity.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day by December 31, 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Florida Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent U.S. Sen. Ashley B. Moody's 94.5% implied probability in the Florida Republican Senate primary reflects her commanding position as Gov. Ron DeSantis' appointee following Marco Rubio's resignation, bolstered by statewide name recognition from her prior attorney general role and key endorsements including Sen. Rick Scott and the Florida Farm Bureau Federation. The April 24 qualifying deadline finalized a field of low-profile challengers—entrepreneur Michaelangelo Hamilton, A.C. Toulme, and podcaster Jake Lang—lacking comparable resources or institutional support, with no recent polls showing primary threats. Traders price in minimal upset risk ahead of the August 18 primary, though scandals, health issues, or a high-profile defection could shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Shevchenko by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Shevchenko, Donetsk Oblast, (48.384157880601364° N, 37.10809761679785° E) between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces have intensified assaults in the Pokrovsk direction of Donetsk Oblast, attempting to dislodge Ukrainian defenders from northern Pokrovsk outskirts and advance toward Shevchenko, a strategically elevated village northwest of the city, with recent reports of armor buildup as of May 2. Ukrainian forces continue repelling attacks near adjacent Rodynske, Hryshyne, and Bilytske, leveraging high ground and drones to inflict heavy Russian casualties amid over 50 daily clashes in the sector. ISW assessments through May 1 confirm no verified Russian territorial gains in Shevchenko itself, reflecting slow progress despite manpower commitments. Traders weigh Ukraine's defensive resilience against mounting pressure from Russian reserves, with no imminent diplomatic breakthroughs; market resolves Yes if ISW maps show any Russian control of Shevchenko territory by the specified date.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump post 60-79 Truth Social posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Donald Trump (@realDonaldTrump), posts on Truth Social between May 5, 12:00 PM ET and May 12, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, Truth Social itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trump's Truth Social posting remains highly unpredictable, with trader consensus pricing tight odds across low (<20 at 26%) and high-volume ranges (160-179 and 180-199 at 26%) for May 5-12, reflecting his variable habits amid presidential duties. Recent data shows subdued activity, averaging 8 posts daily last week (58 total April 25-May 1), including a 20-post spike on May 1 tied to Florida travel, endorsements, and tariff announcements—down from 2025's 18-per-day average. This keeps mid-ranges viable but elevates extremes due to historical late-night barrages exceeding 150 posts. Separation could arise from next week's schedule, breaking news on policy or foreign affairs like Iran, or quiet executive time.

markets.question: AZ vs. FC Twente '65: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between AZ and FC Twente '65, scheduled for May 3 at 10:45 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if AZ and FC Twente '65 combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: AZ vs. FC Twente '65 - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:45 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: AZ Alkmaar hosts FC Twente in a pivotal Eredivisie matchup at AFAS Stadion on May 3, with both clubs vying for European spots—Twente in 4th and AZ in 6th—following strong recent form. Twente impressed with a 2-1 upset victory away at Ajax last weekend, extending their win streak amid a solid away record, while AZ posted a clean-sheet 2-0 home win over Fortuna Sittard, leveraging their strong home advantage where they beat Twente 2-1 in February's KNVB Beker. AZ faces injury concerns with Jizz Hornkamp (ankle), Peer Koopmeiners (muscle), and others sidelined, potentially thinning their attack; Twente misses long-term absentee Mees Hilgers (cruciate). Head-to-head trends suggest a low-scoring affair, with recent encounters decided by single goals.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at 72.5% implied probability to claim the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, reflecting UEFA's dominant allocation of 16 direct qualifiers including powerhouses like France, Spain, Germany, England, Portugal, Netherlands, and recent playoff victors Sweden, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Turkey, and Czechia, whose campaigns underscored the confederation's depth amid the expanded 48-team format. South America's 20.5% share stems from CONMEBOL heavyweights Brazil and Argentina securing spots in the grueling round-robin, positioning them for deep knockout runs despite fewer slots. Asia, Africa, North America, and Oceania trail below 3% each, hampered by historical finals droughts, debutants like Cape Verde and Curaçao, and weaker head-to-head records against top UEFA and CONMEBOL sides. With qualifiers concluding March 31 via tense inter-confederation playoffs and the group draw now set, no major injury or roster shifts have altered this sentiment in recent weeks.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 16°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory recordings show a morning minimum temperature of 23°C so far on May 3 amid moderate northerly winds from a northeast monsoon influence, cloudy skies, heavy showers under an amber rainstorm warning, and severe thunderstorms with hail and gusts—conditions suppressing daytime warming after an early peak near 30°C. Trader consensus implies 54% odds for 23°C as the daily low at the Observatory, reflecting this verified observation, while 39.5% back 22°C anticipating a possible overnight dip from persistent cloud cover and cool air advection, below May's climatological average of 24°C. Inherent short-range forecast uncertainty persists, with the next HKO update at 16:30 HKT and resolution based on the official 24-hour minimum.

markets.question: Will Mistral have the #2 AI model at the end of June 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the second-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies second place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' Kimi Antonelli leads the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship with 75 points after victories in China and Japan, ahead of teammate George Russell's 68 points from consistent podiums including an Australian Grand Prix win, driving trader consensus to price them at 35% and 28% implied probabilities. The tight intra-team battle reflects Mercedes' early-season superiority in qualifying pace and race reliability, but Red Bull's Miami upgrades have Max Verstappen closing the gap to second on the grid behind Antonelli's pole, while McLaren's Lando Norris holds realistic contention via Sprint successes and strong form. With 20-plus Grands Prix remaining, frequent development cycles, tire strategy variables, and DNF risks keep the title race competitive among the top constructors.

markets.question: Will North and South Korea engage in direct talks by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, North and South Korea engage in direct talks, defined as an official meeting or communication between government representatives of both sides, conducted without a third-party relaying messages, by June 30, 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise this market will resolve to “No”.

The talks may be in-person, by phone, or virtual, and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by credible media. 

Routine military deconfliction, backchannel exchanges, or talks conducted entirely through another country or organization will not count. 

The resolutions source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: North Korea's barrage of short-range ballistic missile tests in late April—including launches on April 24 and 19 equipped with cluster munitions—has reinforced trader consensus against direct talks with South Korea by June 30, implying a 93.5% probability of no engagement. While Seoul's unification minister has urged dialogue and sought EU mediation for trilateral talks, North Korea remains unresponsive amid heightened tensions. Late-breaking diplomatic overtures or summits could shift odds, though historical patterns suggest formidable barriers persist.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will Taylor Ward lead the MLB in doubles for the 2026 regular season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the player who hits the most doubles during the 2026 Major League Baseball regular season.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by the rules of the MLB. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player with the higher batting average during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve in favor of the player with the higher slugging percentage during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, then this market will resolve in favor of the player that records more hits during the 2026 MLB regular season. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 MLB regular season is cancelled, postponed after October 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MLB; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's latest short-range forecast, updated May 3 morning, projects Seoul's highest temperature on May 4 at 19°C, with morning rain (60% probability) clearing to afternoon sunshine amid light southwest winds and persistent cloud cover. Trader consensus favors 18°C at 39% implied probability, reflecting caution from recent cooler-than-average early May trends—such as May 3's projected 15–16°C high under similar overcast conditions—and potential suppression of daytime heating by lingering moisture from a passing frontal system. Climatological baselines for early May show averages near 20°C, but model uncertainty grows within 24 hours of resolution based on official station measurements at Incheon International Airport; watch KMA's next hourly updates for refinements.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Pentagon's May 2 announcement of withdrawing approximately 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next six to 12 months has heightened NATO tensions, reflecting President Trump's longstanding criticisms of European allies' burden-sharing amid disputes over support in conflicts like those involving Iran. This repositioning signals de-escalation in US commitments but stops short of full treaty withdrawal, which faces steep legal barriers under Section 1250A of the 2024 National Defense Authorization Act requiring congressional approval—a process historically resistant given bipartisan support for the alliance. Trader sentiment weighs Trump's rhetorical threats against institutional hurdles, with no imminent congressional votes or NATO summits scheduled to force a decision before year-end.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: AC Esports vs Team Rey - Game 1 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between AC Esports and Team Rey in the MPL Malaysia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 3 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "AC Esports" if AC Esports win Game 1 against Team Rey.

This market will resolve to "Team Rey" if Team Rey win Game 1 against AC Esports.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 1 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 1. 

If Game 1 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: AC Esports vs Team Rey (BO3) - MPL Malaysia Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between AC Esports and Team Rey in the MPL Malaysia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 3 at 5:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "AC Esports" if AC Esports win the match against Team Rey.

This market will resolve to "Team Rey" if Team Rey win the match against AC Esports.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Rey enters the Bo3 rematch against AC Esports in MPL Malaysia Season 17 regular season with commanding momentum, sitting 3rd in standings at 5-2 (11-7 games) after a 2-1 head-to-head win over AC on April 26 that capped a five-match streak, bolstered by coach Caleb's emphasis on growing chemistry and trust. AC Esports, mired in 7th at 2-6 (7-13 games), snapped a skid with a 2-0 rout of bottom-dwelling Team Flash on May 1 but faces pressure in the double round-robin format where top-6 teams advance to playoffs. Recent roster tweaks for both sides have stabilized lineups amid patch 2.1.61 meta shifts, heightening stakes for this Week 5 clash.

Nikema Williams commands 97.8% trader consensus to win Georgia's 5th Congressional District Democratic primary on May 19, driven by her fundraising dominance and incumbency advantages in the overwhelmingly Democratic Atlanta-based district. An April 24 Atlanta Journal-Constitution analysis underscored her strong re-election positioning against low-traction challengers Victor Hill—a former Clayton County sheriff with prior legal issues—Arnetress Beatty, and Andres Castro, amid no public polling showing competition. markets.question: Fed rate cut by December 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is decreased at any point between December 16, 2025 and the completion of the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting for December 2026, currently scheduled for December 8-9. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no December meeting takes place by January 7, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No". 

Emergency rate cuts will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If no January meeting takes place by February 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and no qualifying rate cut has been announced, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve's April 28-29, 2026, FOMC meeting held the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75% amid persistent inflation pressures and a resilient labor market, marking the third consecutive pause with notable internal dissent—the highest since 1992. March CPI surged to 3.3% year-over-year, exceeding forecasts after a 0.9% monthly jump, while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs and unemployment held at 4.3%. Chair Powell emphasized a cautious stance, citing geopolitical risks and sticky inflation as barriers to easing. Polymarket trader consensus, reflecting skin-in-the-game probabilities, prices delayed cuts at best, with CME FedWatch implying near-certainty of no change at the June 16-17 meeting. Key catalysts include April CPI on May 12 and April jobs data revisions.

markets.question: Will Hamas agree to disarm by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hamas officially announces it will disarm in the Gaza Strip by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only credible announcements, for example by the widely acknowledged leadership of Hamas, will qualify.

For the purposes of this market, "disarm" refers to a public commitment to relinquish or dismantle its military, whether partially or completely, in the Gaza Strip.

Announcements of partial disarmament (e.g., surrendering a class of weapons or agreeing to disarm in stages or a certain region) will qualify as long as it is part of an acknowledged disarmament process.

Only official announcements will qualify. Informal statements, plans contingent on future conditions, statements of intent without a formal policy directive, or any other statements that do not constitute a formal policy announcement will not be considered.

Primary resolution sources will include official statements from Hamas leadership; however, a wide consensus of credible reporting confirming a policy of disarmament has been instituted will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hamas has rejected multiple disarmament proposals from the US-backed Board of Peace, a key element of President Trump's Gaza framework, insisting any weapons surrender requires full Israeli troop withdrawal, blockade lifting, and progress toward Palestinian statehood. Recent April deadlines expired without agreement, with Hamas offering only partial concessions like surrendering some rifles while its armed wing deems full disarmament a "red line." As of May 2, the group called a new 15-item phase two proposal a negotiation basis but tied disarmament to political settlement talks. Israeli ministers are now discussing resuming Gaza operations amid stalled Cairo-mediated efforts, heightening escalation risks before June 30 milestones. Trader consensus reflects deep impasse, with no confirmed talks advancing demilitarization.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the SC-02 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the SC-02 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Hart Memorial Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NHL. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: NHL Hart Memorial Trophy Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the player who is awarded the 2025–26 NHL Hart Memorial Trophy.

If the listed player is not announced as a finalist for the 2025–26 Hart Memorial Trophy, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Connor McDavid's league-leading 138 points (48 goals, 90 assists)—capped by his sixth Art Ross Trophy—position him as the clear trader consensus at 50% implied probability for the Hart Memorial Trophy, underscoring his carry role for Edmonton with a point in all 41 Oilers regular-season wins amid a tight playoff push. Nikita Kucherov's efficient 130 points (44G-86A) in just 76 games and Tampa Bay's strong Atlantic Division finish sustain his 29% share, while Nathan MacKinnon's Maurice "Rocket" Richard Trophy with 53 goals and Colorado's top Western standing back his 18% bid in this three-horse race. End-of-season NHL.com voting favored Kucherov slightly, but McDavid's raw dominance and late tear drive market favoritism as finalists await announcement around May 8.

markets.question: Will Nationalist Party win the third-most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?
markets.description:
General elections are scheduled to be held in Malta on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results reported by the Government of Malta, such as those published by the Electoral Commission of Malta (https://electoral.gov.mt/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Robert Abela's announcement of a snap parliamentary election on May 30, 2026—a year ahead of schedule—has intensified focus on vote shares, with recent polls driving trader consensus that ADPD (AD+PD) leads for third place at 64.5%. MaltaToday and Times of Malta surveys show Labour at 48-51%, Nationalist Party (PN) at 37-46%, ADPD at 2.6-3.7%, and Momentum at 2.1%, positioning the green-left ADPD ahead of the new Momentum party despite their April 17 candidate coordination pact to avoid vote splitting. Latest Sagalytics and Marmarà polls reinforce Labour's widening lead by 19,000-28,000 votes, marginalizing far-right Imperium Europa and others below 1%, while PN's second-place hold keeps its third-place odds negligible. With the single transferable vote system, final rankings hinge on campaign momentum and turnout in the 27 days ahead.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting trader consensus on a cautious stance amid persistent inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year, up from 2.4% in February, while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs and unemployment held steady at 4.3%, signaling a resilient labor market that tempers rate-cut expectations. The March dot plot projects a gradual decline to around 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027, aligning with market-implied paths from Fed funds futures. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, where hotter data could delay easing further.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 chart dated May 2, 2026, marking her first chart-topper of the year and bringing the total to nine unique artists with No. 1s so far: Mariah Carey (holiday re-entry), Taylor Swift (two hits including "The Fate of Ophelia" and "Opalite"), Bruno Mars ("I Just Might"), Harry Styles ("Aperture"), Ella Langley (seven non-consecutive weeks with dominant country crossover "Choosin' Texas"), Bad Bunny ("DtMF"), BTS ("Swim"), and Rodrigo. Current frontrunners like Olivia Dean ("Man I Need" at No. 4) and climbers such as Justin Bieber's "Daisies" (No. 8) signal streaming-fueled momentum amid fierce competition. Traders eye upcoming releases from heavyweights like Drake, Kendrick Lamar, and Sabrina Carpenter, with weekly Tuesday chart updates and summer album drops as key catalysts through December.

markets.question: Will there be a safety car during the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a safety car is deployed at any point during the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix, scheduled for May 3, 2026.

The market will resolve to "No" if the race is completed without any safety car deployment.

If the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 10, 2026, this market will resolve 50-50.

Virtual Safety Car (VSC) deployments do not count as safety car deployments for the purpose of this market. Only physical safety car deployments where the safety car enters the track will result in a "Yes" resolution.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Severe thunderstorms forecasted for Sunday's 2026 Miami Grand Prix race day, with up to 88% precipitation chance and lightning risks triggering Florida's 30-minute helicopter medevac protocols, have driven the 72% implied probability for a safety car deployment. Miami's tight, wall-lined layout amplifies incident potential in wet conditions, as seen historically with frequent crashes and debris. Yesterday's extended 90-minute FP1, testing 2026 regulation tweaks like reduced ERS deployment and low-power start detection, featured Oliver Bearman's spin and crash, signaling teething issues with the new active aero cars on this unforgiving track. Trader consensus reflects these factors heightening chaos odds despite dry sprint sessions.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

markets.question: Will Bert Mizusawa be the Republican nominee for Senate in Virginia?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Virginia Republican Senate Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for United States Senator from Virginia.

If no 2026 Virginia Republican Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Virginia Republican party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Retired Army Major General Bert Mizusawa leads trader consensus at 52% implied probability to win Virginia's Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 4, reflecting his strongest Q1 fundraising ($192,000 raised, $55,000 cash on hand per VPAP) and national security credentials in a fragmented nine-candidate field challenging incumbent Democrat Mark Warner. David Williams (22%) and Kim Farington (16%) follow, boosted by recent Republican Party of Virginia certification of their ballot qualifiers alongside Mizusawa, confirming official primary access. Absent public polls or major endorsements, the wisdom-of-crowds pricing—backed by $2.4 million in volume—highlights early momentum, though state party conventions or donor shifts could alter dynamics before absentee voting begins.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $74,000 on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

eventMetadata.context_description: The open-seat NY-21 House race, vacated by retiring Rep. Elise Stefanik for her gubernatorial bid, favors Republicans at 64.5% trader consensus due to the district's R+10 partisan lean and historical GOP dominance, including Stefanik's consistent double-digit victories. Recent developments include President Trump's April 21 endorsement of GOP primary frontrunner Anthony Constantino—leading polls and fundraising with $3.5 million cash on hand—over state party-backed Robert Smullen, intensifying the June 23 Republican primary while signaling strong national GOP support. Democrats feature Blake Gendebien with $2.5 million cash but face a steep uphill battle in this Solid Republican-rated district per Cook Political Report, with no general election polls yet available. Primaries could shape nominees' general viability amid national midterm dynamics.

markets.question: Will the NYT front-page headlines say "AI" or "Intelligence" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed term is included in a headline on the New York Times front page between May 4 and May 10, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A headline is defined as the bolded or enlarged text directly preceding each article, previewing the article’s content and typically separated from the article’s text by a black line and byline. The primary headline for each story is the headline for that story with the largest text, typically appearing in bold font and above any other headlines or text for that article.

Sub-headlines, defined as additional bolded or enlarged text not separated from the primary headline by any text, will count, whether they appear before the byline or are partially surrounded by the article text but still adjacent to the primary headline. Pull quotes, however, or any bolded text not adjacent to the primary headline, will not count.

Banner headlines, defined as front-page headlines bordered on the sides only by white space, will count.

Image captions, article text, or any other text that does not constitute a headline, will not qualify.

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variations in capitalization, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will not count.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all slang forms, will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

If the listed term appears as part of a compound word, usage of that compound word qualifies, provided the listed term remains a distinct component of the compound. This does not include suffixes, prefixes, alternative tenses, or grammatical variations that alter the root word. (E.g. if the listed term is joy, killjoy qualifies but joyful does not. E.g. if the listed term is sun, sunflower qualifies but sunny does not.)
If the listed term is part of a hyphenated compound, usage of that hyphenated compound qualifies. (E.g. if the listed term is NATO, pro-NATO and anti-NATO qualify.)

The resolution source for this market will be the New York Times daily newspaper, including images of the front page posted daily at: https://nytimes.pressreader.com/the-new-york-times/

markets.question: Spread: FC Barcelona (-2.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Barcelona" if FC Barcelona win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "FC Bayern München".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. FC Bayern München - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for May 3 at 10:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona Femení hold a commanding position in Liga F with a near-perfect 24-0-1 record, propelled by recent thumpings of Real Madrid including a 3-0 quarter-final win on March 29 to secure their UEFA Women's Champions League semi-final spot against Bayern Frauen. Bayern, unbeaten domestically this season atop the Frauen-Bundesliga save for a 7-1 group-stage drubbing by Barcelona in October 2025, advanced via a gritty 5-3 aggregate over Manchester City with late strikes from Viggósdóttir and Dallmann. First leg on April 25 in Munich favors Bayern's home crowd and revenge motive, but Barcelona's depth shines despite Aitana Bonmatí's ongoing fibula absence until early May; Bayern miss winger Klara Bühl (muscle injury). Head-to-head favors Barça (4-1-0), underscoring trader focus on aggregate dynamics, clean sheets, and Salma Paralluelo's form.

markets.question: SC Internacional vs. Fluminense FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between SC Internacional and Fluminense FC, scheduled for May 3 at 5:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both SC Internacional and Fluminense FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: SC Internacional vs. Fluminense FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 3 at 5:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fluminense's surge to third in the Brasileirão Série A table with 20 points from 10 matches, capped by a 3-1 home win over Corinthians on April 2, underscores their strong recent form and attacking prowess, positioning them as trader favorites despite playing away at Estádio Beira-Rio. Internacional, sitting around mid-table after a 1-1 draw against São Paulo on April 1, benefits from home advantage and an even head-to-head record, but faces injury concerns with wingers Bruno Tabata (head) and Kayky (hamstring) sidelined. Fluminense misses midfielder Gustavo Nonato (ankle) and Facundo Bernal (knee), both expected back mid-April. Upcoming rivalry clashes—Internacional vs. Grêmio and Fluminense vs. Flamengo on April 11—could influence pre-match momentum and squad rotation.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both L1ga Team and REKONIX each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via forfeit, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had killed Roshan prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the U.S. military capture of Nicolás Maduro on January 3, 2026, and his ongoing detention facing charges, Delcy Rodríguez was sworn in as interim president, yet traders imply a 64.5% probability she will not hold the position by December 31, pricing Maduro highest due to persistent institutional recognition including potential UN listings and constitutional claims despite his absence. Rodríguez, at 22.5%, has consolidated power through March military reshuffles sacking Maduro ally Vladimir Padrino López and recent purges, while exceeding her 90-day mandate without elections. Opposition leader María Corina Machado, at 7.5%, plans a year-end return urging polls amid a U.S. bipartisan Senate resolution demanding elections and sanctions relief signals, underscoring unresolved transition pressures and no-confidence risks.

markets.question: Will Khamzat Chimaev be the next fighter to be ranked first in the UFC Pound-For-Pound Rankings in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings, following Islam Makhachev.

If Islam Makhachev remains #1 in the UFC Pound-For-Pound rankings through December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no next UFC fighter to be ranked 1st in the Pound-For-Pound rankings within this timeframe, this market will resolve to “Islam/No Next #1 in 2026”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC (https://www.ufc.com/athletes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Ilia Topuria as the frontrunner at 55.5% implied probability to become UFC's next pound-for-pound #1 in 2026, fueled by his rapid rise to #2 in official rankings after capturing the lightweight title via first-round knockout of Charles Oliveira in June 2025, extending his perfect 17-0 record with highlight-reel finishes over Max Holloway and Alexander Volkanovski. Alex Pereira follows closely at 41.1%, reflecting light heavyweight title defenses and his bold interim heavyweight title pursuit against Ciryl Gane at UFC White House on June 14. Islam Makhachev's entrenched #1 status post-welterweight championship win over Jack Della Maddalena, or no displacement, holds 37%, while Della Maddalena's fresh knockout loss to Carlos Prates in the UFC Perth main event yesterday dents his 25% support. Dricus Du Plessis' 35.2% and Khamzat Chimaev's 19.3% stem from middleweight title intrigue and undefeated streaks.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Singapore be 30°C on May 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Singapore Changi Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 5 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Singapore Changi Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/sg/singapore/WSSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: NEA Singapore's latest 4-day forecast projects a 34°C high for May 5 under inter-monsoon conditions with light WSW winds and afternoon thundery showers, yet traders assign only 2.6% implied probability to 34°C or higher, favoring 33°C at 30.5% amid clustered odds for 30–33°C. This reflects uncertainty from convective activity, where early cloud cover and showers often suppress peaks despite persistent warmth following April's heatwave (12 days above 34°C). Differentiating factors include shower timing over Changi Airport station—late onset allows brief spikes to 34°C, while midday coverage caps at 32–33°C—and high humidity amplifying heat index but limiting surface highs. NEA updates twice daily; historical May averages hover at 31–32°C.

markets.question: Will Lee Jung-hyun win the 2026 Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election?
markets.description:
The Jeonnam–Gwangju mayoral election is scheduled to take place on June 3, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If the result of this election isn't known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the South Korean government, specifically the National Election Commission (https://www.nec.go.kr/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party of Korea candidate Min Hyung-bae commands 98.2% trader consensus in the inaugural Jeonnam-Gwangju Integrated Special City mayoral race due to the region's entrenched status as a Honam stronghold, where his party routinely secures overwhelming majorities in local elections—often exceeding 70%—rooted in historical voting patterns and unified party support following his April 14 primary victory over rivals like Kim Young-rok. People Power Party's Lee Jung-hyun, nominated April 22 despite national experience as former party leader, faces formidable regional barriers, with conservatives historically under 30% there; his May 2 public debate proposal to Min has yet to shift dynamics. While late-breaking scandals, health issues, or a DPK campaign misstep could narrow the gap before the June 3 vote, structural partisan math sustains Min's dominance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner's world No. 1 ranking and blistering 29-2 record in 2026, capped by his Monte-Carlo Masters 1000 title over Carlos Alcaraz on April 12 and ongoing Madrid Open dominance with 18 straight Masters wins, position him as trader consensus favorite at 48% implied probability for the 2026 Wimbledon men's singles crown. Alcaraz, at 19%, holds strong grass-court credentials as a two-time recent champion but trails head-to-head lately after the Monte-Carlo final loss, while Novak Djokovic's lingering shoulder injury—prompting withdrawals from Miami, Monte Carlo, and Madrid—has eroded his odds to 6.8% amid concerns over his fitness heading into grass. Alexander Zverev rounds out the top tier at 3.7% on steady No. 3 form, as pre-grass preparations highlight Sinner's versatile surge and Djokovic's vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 surged to a 52-week high of 7,272 this week on robust Q1 2026 earnings growth topping 12%, led by AI leaders like Alphabet amid double-digit sector expansion, pushing the index to around 7,230. The Federal Reserve's April 29 decision to hold the federal funds rate steady at 3.50%-3.75%—with notable internal dissent—reflects caution over March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, tempering rate-cut expectations while supporting equity valuations. Analyst consensus eyes year-end targets of 7,600-7,900, implying 5-10% upside on projected 17% full-year earnings growth. Traders watch April CPI data due May 12 and the June FOMC for policy shifts influencing risk appetite.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on EUR/USD reaching key 2026 levels hinges on widening policy divergence, with the pair consolidating near 1.17 amid Eurozone inflation rebounding to 3.0% y/y in April flash CPI—up from 2.6% in March—fueling ECB hike speculation despite its April 30 hold at 2.15%. The Fed's federal funds rate remains steady at 3.5%-3.75% (effective 3.64% in April), supporting USD strength exacerbated by Middle East tensions. Eurozone Q1 GDP expanded just 0.1%, with consumer confidence hitting a 3-year low. Watch US April CPI on May 12, FOMC June 16-17, and ECB June policy meeting for catalysts that could push the pair toward extremes.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: New York's 13th Congressional District, with a Cook PVI of D+32, overwhelmingly favors Democrats, driving trader consensus to 93.5% for a Democratic House election winner on November 3, 2026. Incumbent Rep. Adriano Espaillat (D) leads ahead of the June 23 closed Democratic primary, where four lesser-known challengers have filed but face a divided field in this majority-Latino, urban district spanning upper Manhattan and the Bronx. No Republican candidates have emerged, underscoring the lack of general election competition amid massive Democratic voter registration advantages and Espaillat's prior 83%+ margins. A recent April 20 challenger poll highlighted soft incumbent support, yet reinforced primary frontrunner status. Realistic shifts require post-primary scandals, nominee withdrawal, or unprecedented GOP recruitment amid a national Republican wave.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve maintained its federal funds target range at 3.50%-3.75% following the April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting, reflecting trader consensus on a cautious stance amid persistent inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year, up from 2.4% in February, while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs and unemployment held steady at 4.3%, signaling a resilient labor market that tempers rate-cut expectations. The March dot plot projects a gradual decline to around 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027, aligning with market-implied paths from Fed funds futures. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, where hotter data could delay easing further.

markets.question: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC: Draw at halftime?
markets.description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 4, 2026 If the game ends in a draw within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time, this market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No". This market refers only to the outcome within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Chelsea FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: State Rep. Lindsay James commands 92.5% trader consensus to win Iowa's 2nd Congressional District Democratic primary on June 2, reflecting her edge as a multi-term Iowa House member with strong name recognition in Dubuque County, superior fundraising that outpaces rivals, and a key endorsement from the Iowa AFL-CIO announced last week. Recent general election polling from Public Policy Polling (April 13-14) showing her leading presumed GOP nominee Joe Mitchell bolsters her frontrunner status among primary voters seeking a competitive general election candidate. Challengers Kathy Dolter, Clint Twedt-Ball, Don Primus, and Guy Morgan trail with minimal resources and visibility. Upsets remain possible via late major endorsements, a campaign scandal, or unexpected turnout shifts, though structural barriers favor James.
